mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic output that boosts short-term equity momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive alpha vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and higher aggregate income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.03) during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP indicates synchronized expansion across residential and broader economic sectors, driving equity valuations higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when both series rise, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.15) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 1500 thousand units signals strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which drives short-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances where HOUST exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
